name: New York Sour

ingredients.ingredient: Dry red wine (Malbec, Shiraz, or Merlot)

spec.method_detail: Dry shake if using egg. Shake with ice. Strain over fresh ice in rocks glass. Gently float red wine on top over back of spoon.

history: A Whiskey Sour with a red wine float. Known as the 'Continental Sour' in the 1880s Chicago cocktail scene, then later renamed. The wine float creates a stunning visual gradient and adds tannic complexity and fruit to the citrus-sweet-spirit base. It fell out of favor and was revived in the craft cocktail era.
